description.task: Implement search in a Binary Search Tree (BST). Return the node value if found, or -1 if not. Also implement a method to find the k-th smallest element using in-order traversal.
instructions: search: if(root.val==target) return target; if(target<root.val) return search(root.left,target); return search(root.right,target);
kthSmallest: The in-order logic is mostly provided — the left subtree returns early if non-(-1), then count++, then check
BST search is O(h) where h is height — O(log n) for balanced, O(n) worst case
description.hint: search: if(root.val==target)return target; if(target<root.val)return search(root.left,target); return search(root.right,target);

description.task: Implement a FrequencyMap class backed by HashMap that supports O(1) average search. Build a frequency map from an array, then use it to answer queries: count occurrences, find most frequent, find elements appearing exactly k times.
description.hint: build: freq.merge(x,1,Integer::sum); mostFrequent: if(e.getValue()>best){best=e.getValue();bestKey=e.getKey();} appearsExactly: if(e.getValue()==k) result.add(e.getKey());
description.expectedOutput:
Count 5: 3
Count 7: 0
Most frequent: 5
Appears once: [2, 4, 6, 9]

description.task: Implement Interpolation Search, which improves over binary search for uniformly distributed data by estimating the probe position using the target's value. The probe formula: pos = low + ((target - arr[low]) * (high - low)) / (arr[high] - arr[low]).
instructions: After computing pos: if(arr[pos]==target) return pos; if(arr[pos]<target) low=pos+1; else high=pos-1;
For uniform data like {10,20,...,100}, interpolation search finds target in O(log log n) avg
Search 55: not in array (only multiples of 10) → return -1
